title: contacts.resolve: uppercase hex chars rejected — docs say pubkey is hex but case is undocumented
description: cli/src/config.rs Contacts::resolve accepts a raw pubkey only if it is 64 chars AND all chars pass both is_ascii_hexdigit() AND is_ascii_lowercase(). So uppercase hex (e.g. AABBCC...FF) is rejected even though it's valid hex. The pub_id format is hex(SHA-256(verifying_key)) which is always lowercase in code, but there is no documented requirement that callers must use lowercase. Users copying a pub_id from a tool that formats SHA-256 as uppercase (common) will get a silent rejection that looks like 'contact not found'. Fix: normalize to lowercase before the 64-char check, or accept uppercase and normalize.
